title: Rich Savory Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A cultured and cozy Egyptian dish featuring tender lamb in a rich and savory sauce, flavored with black pepper and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the ground cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the lamb is tender, about 1.5 hours.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
